title: Write-guard hook: longest-prefix command resolution for 3-token verbs + pinned test
action: Close the fail-open gap the plan-checker found: resolveCliCommandName in hooks/bee-write-guard.mjs (:155-178) is hardcoded to 2-token shapes — once 3-token commands exist (state.worker.add, reviews.candidate.add), a bash call like 'bee_state.mjs worker add --nickname X' resolves to the bogus name 'state.worker', matches no registry entry, and the guard silently skips validation (documented fail-open). Extend the hook's resolution to the same longest-prefix-over-registry-names rule du-1 added to bee.mjs (mirror the rule; the hook may import it if the hook already imports from bee.mjs/lib, otherwise duplicate with a comment naming the source of truth). Add test_bee_write_guard_hook.mjs cases pinning: (1) a valid 3-token helper call passes schema validation, (2) an invalid 3-token call (missing required flag) is BLOCKED with the schema-guard message — proving fail-open is closed. Vendor the hook to .bee/bin/hooks/ per the onboarding copy_repo_hook flow.

truths: an invalid 3-token bee CLI call (e.g. state worker add missing a required flag) is blocked by the hook, not silently allowed
a valid 3-token call passes the guard unchanged
all existing hook-suite checks pass unchanged
prohibitions: no change to the hook's fail-open policy for genuinely unrecognized shapes

trace.outcome: Extended write-guard hook's resolveCliCommandName to longest-prefix registry matching (mirrors bee.mjs resolveCommand), closing the fail-open gap for 3-token verbs like state.worker.add; vendored to .bee/bin/hooks and pinned with 3 new tests.
trace.verification_evidence: {"red_failure_evidence":"Before the fix, `node .bee/bin/bee_state.mjs worker add --nickname w1 --cell c1 --json=notaboolean` (an invalid 3-token call: --json is boolean-typed) piped through the unmodified hook exited 0 (fail-open) — resolveCliCommandName's hardcoded 2-token shape guessed the bogus command name \"state.worker\" (no such registry entry), so checkCliShape broke out with no denial, silently skipping schema validation entirely. Reproduced via `git stash push -- hooks/bee-write-guard.mjs` then running the fixture harness by hand before restoring.","green_evidence":"After the fix, the same call exits 2 with stderr: bee CLI-shape guard: ... does not match state.worker.add's schema — invalid type, expected boolean (field: json). test_bee_write_guard_hook.mjs: 19 passed, 0 failed (16 pre-existing + 3 new du-6 cases: valid legacy 3-token call passes, invalid legacy 3-token call is BLOCKED, valid dispatcher-shaped 3-token call passes). Full cell verify chain: test_lib.mjs 219 passed/1 failed and test_bee_cli.mjs 80 passed/2 failed, both failures pre-existing and unrelated (templates/bee.mjs and command-registry.mjs/test_bee_cli.mjs drift from sibling cell du-2's in-flight work, confirmed via `bee_reservations.mjs list --active-only` showing du-2-sonnet holding those reservations) — zero failures in any file this cell owns or touches."}
trace.verify_output: test_bee_write_guard_hook.mjs: 19/19 passed (incl. 3 new du-6 pinned 3-token cases). test_lib.mjs: 219 passed, 1 failed (pre-existing, unrelated: templates/bee.mjs vs .bee/bin/bee.mjs vendoring drift from du-2's concurrent in-flight edit, reserved by du-2-sonnet, outside du-6 files). test_bee_cli.mjs: 80 passed, 2 failed (pre-existing, unrelated: unexercised backlog.*/capture.* registry examples, also du-2's in-flight work on command-registry.mjs/test_bee_cli.mjs, reserved by du-2-sonnet).
